package router

import (
	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
	"gorm.io/gorm"
	"log"
	"net/http"
)

// Positive scenario for router_setup_runs_migrations
// Anti-pattern: Router setup function runs database migrations, mixing infrastructure concerns with routing.
func SetupRouter(db *gorm.DB) *gin.Engine {
	// Anti-pattern: running migrations inside router setup
	log.Println("running database migrations...")
	if err := db.AutoMigrate(&User{}, &Product{}, &Order{}); err != nil {
		log.Fatalf("migration failed: %v", err)
	}
	log.Println("migrations completed")

	r := gin.Default()

	r.GET("/users", func(c *gin.Context) {
		var users []User
		db.Find(&users)
		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, users)
	})

	r.GET("/products", func(c *gin.Context) {
		var products []Product
		db.Find(&products)
		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, products)
	})

	r.GET("/orders", func(c *gin.Context) {
		var orders []Order
		db.Find(&orders)
		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, orders)
	})

	return r
}

type User struct {
	ID    uint   `gorm:"primaryKey" json:"id"`
	Name  string `json:"name"`
	Email string `json:"email"`
}

type Product struct {
	ID    uint    `gorm:"primaryKey" json:"id"`
	Name  string  `json:"name"`
	Price float64 `json:"price"`
}

type Order struct {
	ID       uint    `gorm:"primaryKey" json:"id"`
	UserID   uint    `json:"user_id"`
	ProductID uint   `json:"product_id"`
	Quantity int     `json:"quantity"`
}
